rawResults.summary: This Solar Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) establishes that the Purchaser will buy all electricity generated by the Seller's solar system for an initial term of 20 years, with options to extend. The contract sets pricing per kWh, responsibilities for taxes, and terms for installation, operation, and potential termination.

keyTerms.termLength: Twenty (20) years initial term, with up to three (3) additional five (5) year terms
keyTerms.pricePerKwh: Contract Year $/kWh rates to be filled in Exhibit 1
keyTerms.terminationClause: Termination allowed prior to installation under certain conditions; termination payments schedule in Exhibit 1, Section 6

riskFlags.issue: Contract Price escalation or adjustment terms are not specified, and after installation begins, no price adjustments are allowed, which may lock Purchaser into unfavorable pricing.
riskFlags.evidence: After Commencement of Installation, the Contract Price shall not be subject to further adjustment pursuant to Section 5 of Exhibit 1 or otherwise.

riskFlags.issue: Termination payments are required if the Purchaser terminates early, but the amounts are left blank, creating uncertainty about potential financial exposure.
riskFlags.evidence:
Termination Payment Schedule (Exhibit 3, Section 11(b)):
Contract Year Termination Payment ($)
1 [$_____]
2 [$_____]
3 [$_____]
... up to 20 years

riskFlags.issue: Performance guarantee is optional and may not be provided, leaving Purchaser exposed to risk if the system underperforms.
riskFlags.evidence: d. A Performance Guaranty is _____ is not_____ being provided.

riskFlags.issue: Purchaser is responsible for all taxes related to electricity generation, sale, delivery, consumption, and real property taxes, which could be significant and variable over time.
riskFlags.evidence: Purchaser is responsible for: (1) payment of, or reimbursement of Seller, for all taxes assessed on the generation, sale, delivery or consumption of electricity produced by the System or the interconnection of the System to the utility’s electricity distribution system; and (2) real property taxes.

riskFlags.issue: Several costs are excluded from the Contract Price and are the Purchaser’s responsibility, including unforeseen groundwork, upgrades to electrical infrastructure, landscaping, and structural upgrades, which may lead to unexpected expenses.
riskFlags.evidence: Contract Price Exclusions. Unless Seller and Purchaser have agreed otherwise in writing, and except as otherwise provided in Section 2(c) of Exhibit 3, the Contract Price excludes the following: a. Unforeseen groundwork (including excavation and circumvention of underground obstacles). Upgrades or repair to customer or utility electrical infrastructure (including: client or utility service, transformers, substations, poles, breakers, reclosers, and disconnects). b. Snow removal, tree removal, tree trimming, mowing and any landscape improvements. c. Decorative fencing and/or any visual screening materials, decorative enhancements to solar support structures (including: painting, paint matching, masonry/stone work, and any lighting not required to meet the minimum code compliance). d. Removal of existing lighting, light poles, or concrete light post bases. e. Roof membrane maintenance or reroofing work. f. Structural upgrades to the Improvements, including ADA upgrades.

riskFlags.term: System Capacity and Pricing Details Missing
riskFlags.issue: Key details such as system size (capacity) and specific pricing per kWh are left blank, making it difficult to assess financial terms fully.

rawResults.summary: This Solar Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) is a contract between a Purchaser and a Seller for the sale and purchase of solar-generated electricity from a specified solar system installed on the Purchaser's premises. The agreement sets a 20-year initial term with options for three additional 5-year terms, details pricing per kWh, and outlines responsibilities including maintenance, taxes, and ownership of renewable energy credits (RECs).

keyTerms.termLength: Twenty (20) years initial term, with up to three (3) additional five (5) year terms
keyTerms.pricePerKwh: Contract Year $/kWh: Not specified (to be filled in Exhibit 1)
keyTerms.terminationClause: Termination allowed prior to installation under certain conditions; termination payments defined per year in Exhibit 1; Purchaser may terminate for delay in installation after a specified period; no damages for termination under specified conditions

riskFlags.issue: The contract does not specify any escalation rate for the contract price over the 20-year term, which may expose the Purchaser to unexpected price increases or lack of price adjustments for inflation.
The Performance Guaranty is optional and may not be provided, which could leave the Purchaser without assurance of system performance or compensation if the system underperforms.
riskFlags.evidence: d. A Performance Guaranty is _____ is not_____ being provided.

riskFlags.issue: Termination payment amounts per contract year are left blank, creating uncertainty about financial obligations if the agreement is terminated early.
Purchaser is responsible for all taxes related to generation, sale, delivery, or consumption of electricity and real property taxes, which could result in unexpected tax liabilities.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 3, General Terms and Conditions, Section 3(d)(i)
riskFlags.evidence: Purchaser is responsible for: (1) payment of, or reimbursement of Seller, for all taxes assessed on the generation, sale, delivery or consumption of electricity produced by the System or the interconnection of the System to the utility’s electricity distribution system; and (2) real property taxes.

riskFlags.issue: Seller retains ownership of all Renewable Energy Credits (RECs) and incentives, which means Purchaser cannot claim these environmental benefits or associated financial incentives.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 3, General Terms and Conditions, Section 4
riskFlags.evidence: As the owner of the System, Seller is entitled to the benefit of, and will retain all ownership interests in the RECs and Incentives. Purchaser shall cooperate with Seller in obtaining, securing and transferring any and all RECs and Incentives.

riskFlags.issue: The agreement does not specify who is responsible for operation and maintenance (O&M) of the solar system, which could lead to ambiguity in maintenance obligations.
riskFlags.evidence: No explicit clause found in provided contract text specifying operation and maintenance responsibility.

rawResults.summary: This Solar Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) is a contract between a Purchaser and a Seller for the sale and purchase of electricity generated by a solar system installed on the Purchaser's premises. The agreement sets a 20-year initial term with options for up to three additional 5-year terms, specifies pricing per kWh, and outlines responsibilities including operation, maintenance, and taxes. The Seller retains ownership of the system and all renewable energy credits (RECs) and incentives.

keyTerms.termLength: Twenty (20) years initial term with up to three (3) additional five (5) year terms
keyTerms.pricePerKwh: Contract Year $/kWh: Not specified (to be filled in Exhibit 1)
keyTerms.terminationClause: Termination permitted prior to installation under certain conditions; Purchaser may terminate for delay if installation does not commence within a specified number of days; termination payments specified per year in Exhibit 1

riskFlags.issue: The contract does not specify any escalation rate for the price per kWh over the 20-year term, which may expose the Purchaser to price uncertainty or Seller to inflation risk.
riskFlags.evidence:
Contract Year $/kWh 
1 [$_____]
2 [$_____]
3 [$_____]
... 
20 [$_____]

The first Contract Year shall commence on the Commercial Operation Date, and each subsequent Contract Year shall commence on the anniversary of the Commercial Operation Date.

riskFlags.issue: Performance guarantee is optional and may not be provided, leaving the Purchaser without assurance of minimum system performance.
riskFlags.evidence: d. A Performance Guaranty is _____ is not_____ being provided.

riskFlags.issue: Termination payments are required if the agreement is terminated early, potentially resulting in significant financial liability for the Purchaser.
riskFlags.section: Exhibit 1, Pricing, Item 6 and Exhibit 3, Section 11(b)
riskFlags.evidence:
6. Termination Payment Schedule (Exhibit 3, Section 11(b)):
Contract Year Termination Payment ($)
1 [$_____]
2 [$_____]
... 
20 [$_____]

riskFlags.issue: Purchaser is responsible for all taxes related to generation, sale, delivery, or consumption of electricity and real property taxes, which could be significant and increase costs.
riskFlags.evidence: Purchaser is responsible for: (1) payment of, or reimbursement of Seller, for all taxes assessed on the generation, sale, delivery or consumption of electricity produced by the System or the interconnection of the System to the utility’s electricity distribution system; and (2) real property taxes.

riskFlags.issue: Seller retains ownership of all Renewable Energy Credits and Incentives, which may reduce the Purchaser's ability to claim environmental benefits or receive related financial advantages.
riskFlags.evidence: As the owner of the System, Seller is entitled to the benefit of, and will retain all ownership interests in the RECs and Incentives. Purchaser shall cooperate with Seller in obtaining, securing and transferring any and all RECs and Incentives.

riskFlags.issue: Purchaser may terminate if installation has not commenced within a specified number of days after the Effective Date, but the exact number of days is left blank, creating ambiguity.
riskFlags.evidence: If Commencement of Installation has not occurred [_____] days after the Effective Date, Purchaser may terminate this Agreement by providing thirty (30) days’ prior written notice to Seller; provided that this Agreement will not terminate pursuant to this Section 2(d) if Seller achieves Commencement of Installation on or before the end of such thirty (30) day notice period.

rawResults.summary: This Solar Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) outlines the terms under which the Purchaser agrees to buy all electricity generated by a solar system installed and owned by the Seller on the Purchaser's premises. The agreement includes a 20-year initial term with options for up to three additional 5-year terms, pricing per kWh, and responsibilities for operation, maintenance, taxes, and termination conditions.
